compiled_state.title: Kudos system: detection triggers and agent behavioral improvements
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
Epic 2 of 2. Adds snapshot-dependent detection triggers (T1, T2, T4) and updates four sub-agent behavioral contracts. Requires Epic 1 (kudos infrastructure and primary triggers) to be complete first.

CONTEXT: An engineering team member reviewing the session Kudos ledger needs to trust that T1/T2 awards reflect genuine proactive quality work. Without a pre-session test baseline, the team cannot distinguish deliberate pre-existing debt remediation from incidental passes, eroding trust in the reward system. This epic adds the snapshot infrastructure anchoring T1/T2/T4 to pre-session state, and updates four sub-agent contracts to make quality-seeking framing explicit.

APPROACH: Build kudos-snapshot.sh to capture test-gate-status at worktree creation (before any record-test-status.sh invocation). Three snapshot-dependent triggers read from this file. Four sub-agent contract updates are targeted text additions to existing agent definition files.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
SC-E2-1: kudos-snapshot.sh creates .kudos/test-gate-snapshot.json at worktree creation; reads local test-gate-status file (NOT gh CLI/remote CI); uses flock for atomic read; handles absent file with {tests: {}, captured_at: <ISO-8601 UTC>, source: test-gate-status}
SC-E2-2: kudos-snapshot.sh registered in worktree creation sequence BEFORE first record-test-status.sh invocation; verified by registration order in plugins/dso/hooks/
SC-E2-3 (T1 trigger): commit message references bug ticket ID; dso ticket show confirms type=bug AND created_at predates session merge-base; AND snapshot shows associated test as failed/timeout (via .test-index source-file mapping); AND commit fixes the test
SC-E2-4 (T2 trigger): test appears in snapshot as failed or timeout (NOT resource_exhaustion — transient, excluded explicitly); commit transitions it to passed
SC-E2-5 (T4 trigger): git diff $(git merge-base HEAD main) HEAD --name-only (deleted files excluded) shows measurable reduction in duplicate Python block count (Python AST subtree hashing); merge-base failure -> skip
SC-E2-6: completion-verifier.md contract adds UNCERTAIN as valid overall_verdict; sprint orchestrator re-dispatches with opus on UNCERTAIN; opus UNCERTAIN -> blocked flag. COORDINATION: sequence after/coordinate with 6068-cb2d and 53ee-a9ac to avoid conflicts.
SC-E2-7: approach-decision-maker.md framing update: "You may construct a counter-proposal not only when no proposal satisfies done definitions, but also when the highest-scoring proposal represents an architectural pattern you would not want maintained long-term. Name the quality or maintainability concern explicitly. You are the codebase's steward, not just a selection mechanism."
SC-E2-8: doc-writer.md adds: "Write the minimum that would allow a new contributor to understand the project without asking someone who built it. Prefer accuracy over coverage — an incomplete but accurate document is better than a complete but stale one."
SC-E2-9: using-lockpick/SKILL.md adds kudos system awareness section (active triggers, point display, jitter interpretation)

SCENARIO ANALYSIS:
- Empty snapshot permanently disables T1/T2 on clean worktrees (medium, runtime): expected behavior, document in CLAUDE.md
- Concurrent sessions share test-gate-status file (high, runtime): flock mitigates; document known limitation for simultaneous parallel sessions
- T4 merge-base fails with no common ancestor (medium, runtime): graceful skip, already spec'd
- T1 false award from unrelated bug ticket reference (medium, runtime): mitigation is requiring commit to actually fix the test (SC-E2-3 condition d)

DEPENDENCIES:
- Hard: 3e9b-afee (Epic 1 - kudos infrastructure and primary triggers)
- Soft coordination: 6068-cb2d and 53ee-a9ac (for SC-E2-6 completion-verifier UNCERTAIN verdict)

comments.body:
PREPLANNING_CONTEXT — Epic 71df-5bd8

## Story Map

| Story | ID | P | Description |
|-------|-----|---|-------------|
| S2 (spike) | dadd-0bf8 | 1 | Verify per-test status persistence design for T1/T2 snapshot schema |
| J | 8f02-60b8 | 1 | kudos-snapshot.sh — capture pre-session test state at worktree creation |
| M | 380e-6baa | 2 | Agent contract updates — completion-verifier UNCERTAIN, approach-decision-maker, doc-writer |
| K | 845b-232e | 2 | T1/T2 snapshot triggers — pre-existing bug fix + previously-failing test remediation |
| L | 9fbe-c914 | 2 | T4 trigger — duplicate Python block reduction |
| O | 6800-94ed | 2 | Sprint SKILL.md UNCERTAIN verdict routing — opus re-dispatch loop |
| N | d99c-5fb8 | 3 | using-lockpick/SKILL.md kudos awareness section |
| H2 | dbcc-23de | 4 | CLAUDE.md + operational docs update |

## Dependency Graph

```
dadd-0bf8 (S2)  380e-6baa (M)
     |                |
8f02-60b8 (J)   6800-94ed (O)
     |    |           |
845b (K) 9fbe (L)     |
     |    |           |
     +--d99c (N)------+
              |
         dbcc-23de (H2)
```

## Walking Skeleton

S2 (dadd-0bf8) → J (8f02-60b8) → K (845b-232e, T2 as first trigger)

Minimal end-to-end: snapshot infrastructure → snapshot-dependent trigger (T2: suite failed at worktree creation → commit fixes it → kudos awarded)

## Adversarial Review Summary

Red team: 19 findings (3 critical, 7 high, 8 medium, 1 low)
Blue team: 6 accepted, 13 rejected
Artifact: /tmp/workflow-plugin-8c20e0fd310e4beb/adversarial-review-71df-5bd8.json

Accepted findings applied:
- RT-01 → New Story O (sprint UNCERTAIN handling)
- RT-03 → New Story S2 (snapshot schema spike); J depends on S2
- RT-04 → Story J done defs: registration mechanism via kudos.snapshot_enabled config key
- RT-13 → Story K done defs: T1 source file identification via commit diff + .test-index
- RT-14 → Story K: resolved by S2 spike (both RT-03 and RT-14 are same data gap)
- RT-17 → Story K and L done defs: triggers integrated into commit-validate.sh

## Success Criteria Coverage

SC-E2-1: J (snapshot schema), S2 (schema decision)
SC-E2-2: J (registration mechanism)
SC-E2-3: K (T1 trigger + source file identification)
SC-E2-4: K (T2 trigger)
SC-E2-5: L (T4 trigger)
SC-E2-6: M (completion-verifier UNCERTAIN) + O (sprint orchestrator UNCERTAIN routing)
SC-E2-7: M (approach-decision-maker framing)
SC-E2-8: M (doc-writer principle)
SC-E2-9: N (SKILL.md awareness)

## Escalation Policy: Escalate unless confident

All stories carry consideration notes for escalation points (safeguard files, coordination deps, schema decisions).
comments.author: Claude
